documents.page_content:
Skip To Main Content
What can we help you find today?
Popular Searches
In This Section
Home
Welcome to Conejo Valley Unified School District!
CVUSD News
View All News
CVUSD Celebrates Lunar New Year
February 17, 2026, will usher in the Lunar New Year for 2026 - the Year of the Horse. CVUSD is committed to honoring and valuing the traditions and cultural significance of the Lunar New Year and the contributions and accomplishments of the Asian and Pacific Islander communities. Staff, students and community members are encouraged to learn about the history of Lunar New Year and celebrate with purposeful activities.
Westlake High Earns 2nd Place in 2026 Ventura County Academic Decathlon
Congratulations to our Westlake High School Warriors on earning 2nd place in the 2026 Ventura County Academic Decathlon! The annual event, themed “The Roaring Twenties,” took place last month, with winners announced on February 9th. Nine high schools from across Ventura County participated with students from both Westlake High and Thousand Oaks High earning individual awards and recognitions.
Sustaining Student Mental Health & Wellness
A new Initiative from the State of California, theCalifornia Youth Behavioral Health Initiative (CYBHI), has made it possible for our CVUSD schools to directly be reimbursed by private health insurance plans for the mental health and wellness services provided to students on campus by our mental health clinicians and counselors. Learn how to participate in CYBHI, at no cost to you, and provide critical financial support for these on-campus services.
A “Bite of Reality:” CVUSD High School Students Participate In Financial Literacy Program with Community Partner Logix
Approximately 175 CVUSD high school students completed a financial literacy program Bite of Reality® at a special event hosted by Logix Federal Credit Union. Bite of Reality is an exercise that simulates a real-life experience, by way of an app, complete with financial decisions, bills, and even credit scores.
Two Westlake High Scholars Selected for Competitive Amgen Biotechnology Internship
Congratulations to Westlake High School 10th grade students Smrithi and Amelia for being selected for the highly competitive BOLT! (Biotechnology Opportunities for Leadership and Talent) Internship program through the AMGEN Biotech Experience. This is a prestigious student-focused program for high schoolers and undergraduates designed to explore the diverse and dynamic fields within biotechnology.
It's National School Counseling Week: February 2nd-6th
School Counselors have a vital role in the academic, social, emotional, and career development of students. During National School Counseling Week, CVUSD celebrates the role of school counselors in removing barriers to student learning and providing all students the opportunity to succeed.
CVUSD Celebrates National Black History Month
This February CVUSD celebrates National Black History Month, a time to honor and commemorate the roles African Americans have played in our country's history. The first Black History Week was celebrated in 1926. To mark the 100-year legacy of honoring Black history, culture, and achievements the theme for the 2026 celebration is, “A Century of Black History Commemorations.”
Join Us for the February Parent/Guardian Workshop Event: "Breaking the Silence: Mental Health in Latino Families"
Join us on Monday, February 2nd at 6pm for a free event for CVUSD parents/guardians, which will focus on understanding the stigma surrounding mental health within Latino families and explore ways to better support our students.
You're Invited: Black History Month Celebration
The CVUSD, in proud partnership with the African American District Advisory Council (AADAC), cordially invites all CVUSD families to our Black History Month Celebration. This evening is dedicated to honoring the profound and enduring contributions African Americans have had and continue to make on our culture, history and community.
CVUSD Recognizes International Holocaust Remembrance Day: January 27, 2026
International Holocaust Remembrance Day is a day to remember the millions of lives lost during the Holocaust. We encourage all members of our community to take a moment to reflect on the importance of tolerance, empathy, and compassion, and to remember the victims of the Holocaust.
Sequoia Student Turns Science Fair Win Into Grant to Expand Opportunities for Peers
After earning First Place in the Environmental Sciences category at the 2025 Ventura County Science Fair, Sequoia Middle School eighth-grader Ammon Grano is using his success to open doors for other students by launching and securing support for the school’s first-ever Sequoia Science Fair Club.
Un Evento Gratuito Presentado en Español: Entre Pantallas y Aprendizaje
El Programa de Enlace Comunitario invita a los padres y guardianes de CVUSD a asistir a una presentación especial diseñada para garantizar que la tecnología apoye el aprendizaje sin convertirse en una adicción.
CVUSD TK Winter Wonderland Night
Do you have a child who will be turning 4 by September 1, 2026? Join us for an enchanting evening at our Transitional Kindergarten (TK) Night! CVUSD and Thousand Oaks Library are partnering to create a Winter Wonderland where your child can play, create, and explore while you get a firsthand look at how CVUSD’s TK program builds the foundation for academic success for students.
Supporting Student Success in Math: CVUSD Parent Webinar Events
Our Instructional Services Team invites families to participate in two upcoming CVUSD Math Parent Webinars, designed to support student success in mathematics at every grade level. Two webinars will be hosted - one for elementary families and one for secondary families. Register to attend now.
Newbury Park High, Thousand Oaks High & Westlake High Named to Advanced Placement School Honor Roll
Congratulations to our three comprehensive high schools, which were all named to the 2025 AP School Honor Roll! The AP School Honor Roll recognizes schools whose AP programs are delivering results and broadening participation for students.
Bike Rodeo & E-Bike Safety Event at Redwood Middle School
The free, family-friendly Bike Rodeo & E-Bike Safety Event will take place on Saturday, January 24th from 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. at Redwood Middle School and is designed to help kids and families learn about essential bike and e-bike safety through hands-on activities and educational resources.
Thousand Oaks High’s Lila P. Recognized as Exceptional Teen Scientist, Selected as Regeneron Science Talent Search Top 300 Scholar
Congratulations to Lila Lewis Papavasiliou, a 12th-grade student at Thousand Oaks High School, on being named a Regeneron Science Talent Search Top 300 Scholar. This marks the third consecutive year a student from the school’s Center for Advanced Studies and Research has earned this prestigious national honor.
Congratulations to CVUSD’s January 2026 Teacher of the Month: Lisa Ryder of Westlake High School!
Lisa Ryder is an advocate for all students. She focuses on students’ academic and social-emotional needs, while providing a supportive space where they feel comfortable in asking questions, taking risks and ultimately growing as scholars. A teacher at Westlake High for more than 25 years, we are proud to call her our January 2026 Teacher of the Month!
Join the Conversation: County of Ventura Unlocking Land for Housing Study
The CVUSD in partnership with the County of Ventura is hosting a community meeting on Tuesday, January 20th from 6-7:30 p.m. to gather community input on a site (1342 E. Janss Road) identified in the Unlocking Land for Housing study.
Westlake High Football Coach Rick Clausen Named LA Rams Don Shula Coach of the Year
Congratulations to Westlake High School Head Football Coach Rick Clausen on being selected as the Los Angeles Rams Don Shula Coach of the Year for his character, integrity, leadership, dedication to the community, commitment to player health and safety and on-field success.
Join Us for the Upcoming Parent/Guardian Virtual Workshop
Please join the CVUSD's BreakThrough Student Assistance Program and The Conejo Schools Foundation for a free virtual workshop presented by Laura Stack of Johnny's Ambassadors: What Every Parent Should Know About Marijuana and Psychosis, on Thursday, January 29th, from 6:00 - 7:30 PM.
Now Streaming: Watch Our High School Home Athletic Events Live on Hudl
We are thrilled to announce that our students’ home games at Newbury Park High, Thousand Oaks High and Westlake High will now be live-streamed through HUDL! This exciting new program is made possible through the generous support of CVUSD and our amazing Booster programs.
We're Hiring Paraeducators - Join Us for a Walk-In Interview on January 14th
We're hiring district and substitute paraeducators for Levels I, II, and III. This important role supports students with disabilities. Join us for a Walk-In Interview available on January 14th at our District Operational Center located at 750 Mitchell Road in Newbury Park.
Annual Reorganization of the Board of Education: New President, Vice President & Clerk
At the December 17th Board of Education Meeting, the annual Reorganization of the Board of Education took place. Through their nomination and election process, Board members were voted into the following office positions Karen Sylvester, President, Lauren Gill, Vice President and Bill Gorback as Clerk.
Westlake High Student Wins 2025 Congressional App Challenge for CA’s 26th Congressional District
Congratulations to Kevin Fei, Westlake High 11th grade student, who was announced by Congresswoman Julia Brownley as the winner of the 2025 Congressional App Challenge for California’s 26th Congressional District.  Kevin created the app Motio, which promotes safe driving by providing real-time analysis and input on individual driving performance.
Showing Up is Always in Season: A Holiday Message from CVUSD's Superintendent
This holiday season, I share my utmost gratitude for you: our community of learners, teachers, staff members, families, volunteers and partner organizations who, together, make our school district the exceptional place it is to learn and grow. Excellence in the CVUSD takes shape in so many forms. Yet, none of it would be possible without the most important first step: showing up.
We Proudly Recognize Our Eleven 2025 CVUSD Inclusion Heroes!
In celebration of National Inclusive Schools Week, the CVUSD received a record 140 community nominations honoring our staff members who champion inclusion. We proudly present the 11 outstanding staff members selected as 2025 CVUSD Inclusion Heroes.
You’re Invited: High School Instructional Materials Preview Webinar
The CVUSD community is invited to join the District’s Instructional Services team for two scheduled Preview Nights (virtual) to review  proposed high school instructional materials.
Leadership in Action: Conejo Academy Students Give Back
Congratulations to six students from our Conejo Academy of Leadership and Language Immersion who used creative thinking to recognize a community need, developed a plan for how it could be addressed, and successfully received a grant through the Time for Change Youth Challenge to support their causes!
Congratulations Natalie Taylor of Westlake Elementary: CVUSD’s December 2025 Teacher of the Month!
Natalie Taylor is a true cornerstone of the Westlake Elementary School community who constantly opens doors of opportunity for every learner by recognizing and embracing their diverse needs and goals.
CVUSD Celebrates Inclusive Schools Week!
The week of December 8-12, 2025 is Inclusive Schools Week, an annual event nationally recognized that provides an opportunity for our schools to discuss and celebrate how to successfully include and educate all children. This year's theme is Children First: Building A Stronger Tomorrow.
Congratulations to Westlake High Senior Oscar Ruiz: A 2025 QuestBridge National College Match Recipient!
Congratulations to Westlake High School 12th grade student Oscar Ruiz who has earned a full-ride scholarship to Bowdoin College through the prestigious QuestBridge National College Match Program! This program connects high-achieving students with top-tier colleges that offer full scholarships covering tuition, housing, meals, books, and more.
Inquiry-Based Learning Ahead
Our Sequoia Middle is a Candidate School for the International Baccalaureate (IB) Middle Years Programme (MYP) and Newbury Park High is working to expand its IB World School authorization to grades 9 and 10. Achieving authorization as an IB MYP school involves a comprehensive, multi-year process that demands careful planning, ongoing evaluation, and demonstrated commitment to IB standards.
CVUSD Announces Exciting Lineup of Winter Performing Arts Events
As winter draws near, we’re thrilled to announce the upcoming performance dates for CVUSD’s middle and high school performing arts programs. We invite you to come celebrate the arts with us and cheer on our student performers.
Community Service Opportunity: Apply to Serve on CVUSD's New Conejo Valley Adult Education Advisory Committee
The Conejo Valley Unified School District Adult Education program is looking for members of the local business community and those with experience or education with career technical programs to serve on an advisory board.
2025 Holiday Toy Drive
We're proud to partner with Safe Passage Youth Foundation and the Conejo Schools Foundation for the annual holiday toy drive to benefit CVUSD students of all ages. Donate a new, unwrapped toy at our District Educational Center.
Join Us in Welcoming Adrian Facio, CVUSD's New Risk Manager!
The Conejo Valley Unified School District is pleased to announce that Adrian Facio has been selected as the District’s new Risk Manager. Mr. Facio brings extensive experience in safety, risk management and compliance across multiple industries.
Now Accepting Nominees: 2025 CVUSD Inclusion Heroes!
In honor of National Inclusive Schools Week (December 8-12), CVUSD would like to celebrate Inclusion Heroes at the December 17, 2025 Board of Education meeting. Nominate a teacher, staff member or administrator in recognition of their dedication to creating a community of inclusivity, kindness, and respect, which enhances learning for ALL students.
The Conejo Schools Foundation Supports CVUSD Teachers with More than 285 Grants
The CSF Teacher Grants help to support student success by funding innovative projects that enhance student learning. This year, 286 teachers from 30 CVUSD school sites have been awarded CSF Teacher Grants, each teacher applied for a grant up to $300.
Congratulations to CVUSD’s November 2025 Teacher of the Month: Maribel Reyes of Sequoia Middle!
Maribel Reyes is a true community builder, she works hard to create partnerships between home and school, which is one of the many reasons students in her class thrive. A Science Teacher at Sequoia Middle School since 2020, Ms. Reyes is a master of curriculum standards, NGSS guidelines and is actively embracing the IB framework.
Join us for the November Workshop: Brain Under Construction - A Tool Box to Help Kids Move From Worry to Wellness
All CVUSD Parents/Guardians are invited to this engaging and insightful workshop focused on understanding student anxiety and how knowledge of the brain can help families support children’s emotional well-being and academic success.
Now Open: Enrollment for the 2026-2027 School Year
Enrollment for new CVUSD students for the 2026–2027 school year is officially open! Start your child on their path of educational excellence: complete the Neighborhood School Enrollment process available as of November 3, 2025 - the same date the School Choice Application and Interdistrict Transfer Form opens.
Community Service Opportunity: Our Measure I Bond Oversight Committee Seeks Senior Citizens' Organization Representative
The Conejo Valley Unified School District is seeking an interested, qualified individual to serve on its Independent Citizens’ Bond Oversight Committee as a Senior Citizens' Organization representative. Application submittal deadline: December 1, 2025, at noon.
CVUSD Celebrates Native American Heritage Month
Native American Heritage Month is observed annually in the United States during November to raise awareness of our nation's original inhabitants and descendants' culture, traditions, and achievements.
CVUSD Celebrates National School Psychology Week
CVUSD is proud to celebrate National School Psychology Week from November 3rd to 7th. From fostering success in the classroom to promoting well-being at home and in life, this week is a time to show appreciation for the incredible school psychologists who make a difference every day.
Webinar Recording Now Available: TK Information Night
Is your child turning four? Big news - they are eligible to enroll in TK for the 2026-2027 school year. Watch a recording of our recently held TK Enrollment Information Meeting to learn more about the TK experience, enrollment processes and more!
Aspen Elementary Principal Dr. Shane Craven Honored with Autism Society of Ventura County Award
Congratulations to Aspen Elementary School Principal, Dr. Shane Craven for earning the recognition of Educator of the Year, Integrated Setting from the Autism Society of Ventura County! He was publicly honored with this distinction at the 2025 Awesome in Autism Awards ceremony.
Five CVUSD Schools Earn "Honor Roll School" Recognition
Congratulations to our Acacia Magnet School for Enriched Learning, Lang Ranch Elementary, Maple Elementary, Sycamore Canyon School and Walnut Elementary on being recognized as 2025 Educational Results Partnership (ERP) Honor Roll Schools for their high academic achievement and student success.
Promoting Healthy Lifestyles: CVUSD Celebrates Red Ribbon Week
Red Ribbon Week, celebrated October 23-31, provides an opportunity to discuss drug-free choices and positively model for students by encouraging parents, staff, and students to wear symbolic red ribbons to make a visible statement that we are strongly committed to live a healthy life!
Wear Orange this Wednesday in Support of Unity Day!
The Conejo community is encouraged to wear orange on October 22nd to send a visible message of commitment to kindness, acceptance and inclusion! Unity Day has been celebrated in the United States and around the world since 2011.
CVUSD Proudly Celebrates Week of the School Administrator: October 12th-18th!
This Week of the School Administrator, we are proud to honor our exceptional School Administrators and their invaluable contributions to our students, schools, and community.
Congratulations to CVUSD’s October 2025 Teacher of the Month: Rose Crystal of Acacia Magnet School!
Rose Crystal is a first grade teacher at Acacia Magnet School for Enriched Learning, where, for the last decade, she has been ensuring that each child receives the instruction and encouragement they need to thrive.
Register Now: "Reality Party for Parents" a FREE Event on October 11th
The “Reality Party” is an interactive event in a local home where youth actors portray a teen party to spark meaningful conversations among parents and guardians about real-life challenges and strategies for keeping youth safe and healthy. Register now - spots are limited.
Join Us: Transitional Kindergarten Virtual Information Night
Register to attend our Transitional Kindergarten Information Night on Tuesday, October 28th at 6pm. This online event will share information about the TK learning experience, enrollment processes, child care opportunities available on our CVUSD campuses and more!
Enrollment for the 2026-2027 School Year Opens November 3rd
In the CVUSD we are proud to offer a right fit for every student in our award-winning TK-12 schools. New for the 2026-2027 school year: Neighborhood School Enrollment for \new\ CVUSD students will open on Monday, November 3, 2025 - the same date that the School Choice Application and Interdistrict Transfer Application opens.
Padres y tutores de CVUSD: ¡Los invitamos a aprender inglés con nosotros!
Our CVUSD Parent Outreach Program is once again offering FREE English classes to CVUSD Parents/Guardians. Classes, offered weekly, begin on October 8th. Register now!
CVUSD's Annual College & Career Information Night Returns October 6th
The annual CVUSD Districtwide College & Career Information Night for ALL CVUSD high school students and their families returns on Monday, October 6th from 5:00pm to 7:50pm at Westlake High School!
This October CVUSD Supports National Bullying Prevention Month
This October, the goal of recognizing National Bullying Prevention Month in the CVUSD is to encourage parents, students, and staff to unite against bullying, promote kindness and inclusion, and share the message that no one deserves to be bullied.
CVUSD Recognizes October as LGBTQ+ History Month
LGBTQ+ History Month is observed every year in the United States during October to raise awareness of the history of LGBTQ+ people and related civil rights movements.
Thousand Oaks, Newbury Park and Westlake High Schools are National Banner Special Olympics Unified Champion Schools!
This prestigious honor, awarded by Special Olympics North America, recognizes schools that go above and beyond in creating inclusive environments where students learn, play, and lead together.
Congratulations to CVUSD’s Nine National Merit® Scholarship Program Semifinalists!
Congratulations to the nine CVUSD high school students who were announced as Semifinalists in the 2026 National Merit® Scholarship Program, which honors individual scholars who show exceptional academic ability and potential for success in rigorous college studies.
CVUSD Supports California High School Voter Education Weeks
Did you know that California residents ages 16 and 17 who want to be future voters can pre-register to vote? California High School Voter Education Weeks are: September 15-26, 2025 and April 16-27, 2026 - making it a great time to take advantage of the Pre-Register to Vote opportunity.
Congratulations to CVUSD’s September 2025 Teacher of the Month: Kyle Scalise of CVHS!
Kyle Scalise brings out the best in Conejo Valley High School students. A Social Science teacher at the school for eight years and counting, he initiates relevant intellectual rigor and helps all students meet their full potential, while making learning fun.
CVUSD Celebrates National Hispanic Heritage Month
National Hispanic Heritage Month, September 15th to October 15th, is a time to celebrate the achievements, contributions, histories, traditions, and cultural diversity of Hispanic/Latino/Latinx Americans.
Join Us for the September Parent/Guardian Workshop: School Success – Supporting Your Child’s Academic Journey
Join us for a free Parent/Guardian workshop on September 29th at 6pm. This event is designed to equip families with practical strategies to support their child's learning and overall academic growth.
September is Attendance Awareness Month
September 1, 2025, marks the start of Attendance Awareness Month! This is a time to focus on the importance of attending school daily. CVUSD acknowledges that good attendance matters, and improving attendance and reducing chronic absenteeism takes commitment and collaboration.
Student Volunteer Internship Opportunity with CVUSD's Communication Team
CVUSD high school students who are currently enrolled in the 11th and 12th grade and have an interest in graphic design, video production, social media content creation, photography, website design and writing/journalism are encouraged to apply.
A Message from the Superintendent: Welcome to the 2025-2026 School Year
Welcome to the start of the 2025-2026 school year! We are kicking off this school year with a new Board of Education developed and approved mission statement, which encapsulates the heart of what we do in the Conejo Valley Unified School District.
Now Available: Chromebook Accidental Damage Protection
We are proud to provide all students with a Chromebook through the District’s 1:1 Program. To support this program, CVUSD is offering a Chromebook Accidental Damage Protection (ADP) program for families to eliminate the concern and costs of repairing or replacing damaged, or stolen Chromebooks.
Mobile Devices Policy Update for the 2025-2026 School Year
The Conejo Valley Unified School District Board of Education recently approved an updated version of CVUSD Board Policy 5131.8 “Mobile Communication Devices,” which goes into effect this 2025-2026 school year. Stay informed and review the updated policy now.
Student Injuries & Insurance: 2025-2026 School Year
The safety of our students is of critical importance to all of us and we want to protect them from injury. Even so, accidents do happen (at school and elsewhere) and required medical care can be expensive. Please know that your school does not assume responsibility for such costs but does offer you access to several student accident insurance plans for voluntary purchase.
CVUSD Families: Complete the Required Annual Updates for YOUR Student
We look forward to welcoming your students back for the 2025-2026 school year on Wednesday, August 20th. As with each new school year, required Annual Updates must be completed via the online Q Parent Connection by parents/guardians prior to the first day of instruction.
A Voice for Students: Meet Finn Kerns, Student Board Trustee for the 2025-2026 School Year
Mr. Kerns is a student at Thousand Oaks High School with a passion for public service. Mr. Kerns believes that public education is the most important function of government, and is honored to have this opportunity to help ensure all CVUSD students thrive.
New CVUSD Text Number Launching August 2025: Save 805-491-6616 for Outreach Messages
Starting August 2025, our CVUSD and school site Outreach text messages will be sent using a local 10-digit phone number. New Text Message Number for Outreach:  805-491-6616. We recommend saving this number in your contacts as “[CVUSD/School Name] Alerts” so you always recognize messages from us.
CVUSD Cafeterias to Go Cashless for 2025-2026 School Year
Beginning with the 2025-2026 school year, all CVUSD school cafeterias will transition to a cashless payment system. Students will need to use funds preloaded onto their Parent Connect accounts to make all a la carte cafeteria purchases.
CVUSD Families: Please Complete the "More than a Meal" Application
The 2025-2026 Free / Reduced Meal Application affects a variety of areas unrelated to school meals, including school funding, college application fees, SAT fees, bus passes and more. Even if your child does not plan to eat school lunches, it is important to fill out this online form to help ensure our schools and families receive the available resources.
Join Us for a Walk-In Interview - We’re Hiring Child Care Leaders/Assistants and Preschool Assistant Teachers!
Whether you're an experienced educator or just beginning your journey in child development, make a difference, build your career and grow with us at CVUSD! Join us for a Walk-In Interview event being held on four upcoming dates at our University Center in Thousand Oaks.
FREE Summer Meals Available to ALL Children Ages 2-18
Beginning on Monday, June 16th, our Child Nutrition team is proud to provide summer lunch meals at no cost via meal distribution locations across the Conejo Valley and beyond. All children 2-18 years of age are eligible to receive FREE meals.
2025 Summer Work Permit and Transcript Requests
Work permits will be processed by the District Office from June 23 – August 1, 2025, on Tuesdays and Thursdays each week. All transcript requests must be processed through Parchment.com.
CVUSD Summer Enrichment Learning Resources
Our Instructional Services team has gathered a comprehensive round-up of educational activities that review and help children practice skills in math, language arts, reading and more through engaging games and online programs.
We're Hiring Paraeducators - Join Us for a Walk-In Interview this July!
We are hiring a variety of full-time, part-time and substitute Paraeducators. This important role supports students with disabilities. Join us for a Walk-In Interview available on July 9th and July 16th at our District Educational Center.
Upcoming Events
View All Events
Holiday - President's Day (No School)
all day
CVUSD Celebrates Lunar New Year
all day
CVUSD School and Community Resource Fair for Families of Students with IEPs
Board of Education Meeting
CVUSD Math: Parent Webinar
Student District Advisory Committee (SDAC) Meeting
District English Learners Advisory Committee (DELAC) Meeting
African-American District Advisory Council (AADAC)
Black History Month Celebration Event
Special Education District Advisory Council (SEDAC) Meeting
Recent Highlights
See What’s Happening At CVUSD
Mission & Resources
CVUSD Forward
> Committed to providing exceptional educational experiences for all students, the CVUSD focuses on offering opportunities and choices that foster each student's unique gifts and prepares them to reach their full potential for success in a diverse, challenging, and changing world.
Watch our Informational Video to learn more:
Community Rights and Resources
 Nondiscrimination Statement
 Nondiscrimination/Harassment Policy 5145.3
 Nondiscrimination/Harassment Regulation 5145.3
 Bullying Policy 5131.2
 Bullying Regulation 5131.2
 California Department of Education Bullying Resources
 Sexual Harassment Policy 5145.7
 Sexual Harassment Regulation 5145.7
 Sexual Harassment Complaint Procedures 5145.7
 Suicide PreventionPolicy 5141.52
 Suicide Prevention Reguation 5141.52
 Discrimination and Harassment Based on Sex Definition
Local Control Accountability Plan
Click Here to View the CVUSD Local Control Accountability Plan
AII CVUSD learners are served by a set of related plans that work in conjunction to implement our four districtwide LCAP goals. Optimal learning, equitable access, and positive student outcomes are the primary drivers of all four district goals.
Award-Winning Schools
Committed to providing exceptional educational experiences for all students, the CVUSD focuses on offering opportunities and choices that foster each student’s unique gifts and prepares them to reach their full potential for success in a diverse, challenging, and ever-changing world.
Learn More about Award-Winning Schools
A "Right Fit" School for Every Student
From the District’s high-achieving neighborhood schools to its magnet schools and unique program offerings, there are opportunities to match every student’s unique interests in the Conejo!
Learn More about A "Right Fit" School for Every Student
Caring Learning Environments with Exceptional Educators
What sets the CVUSD apart are its consistently top-ranked educators, dedicated administrators and wonderful staff members who are deeply dedicated to scholarly excellence and cultivating caring campus communities.
Learn More about Caring Learning Environments with Exceptional Educators
Co-Curricular Success: Athletics & Activities
CVUSD’s schools are home to top-performing athletic programs that consistently earn county and state recognition with scholar-athletes going on to play collegiate-level sports, year after year. There are also a wide variety of club opportunities for students to join, gain new skills and have fun, all while making connections with like-minded peers.
Learn More about Co-Curricular Success: Athletics & Activities
A Focus on Arts Education
Arts education is a priority for CVUSD scholars with a comprehensive districtwide Strategic Arts Education Plan, which lays the foundation for integrated arts into core content areas including visual arts, theater, media arts and music opportunities for all scholars.
Learn More about A Focus on Arts Education
Preparing Students for the Future of Their Dreams
CVUSD prepares students for achieving the future of their dreams. CVUSD graduates not only attend some of the top universities and colleges in the world, but they also learn skills to prepare them for careers and vocational opportunities after graduation day.
Learn More about Preparing Students for the Future of Their Dreams
What are you waiting for?
Discover Unlimited Opportunities

District 5 Newsletter April 2026
Dear D5 residents,
Get ready for this year’s State of the City at Surprise Stadium, where the community will come together to hear about the progress, achievements and future plans shaping Surprise. The event promises an exciting look at where we are and where we’re headed next. For more information about State of the City and other events, please take a moment to read my newsletter. Thank you!
Inside This Issue
Current Events
   Landscape & Water Efficiency Workshops
   Surprise Day of Service Event

Save the Date

CURRENT EVENTS
Landscape & Water Efficiency Workshops
Water Conservation classes are being offered to help residents understand desert adaptive plants and how to properly take care of them. Classes are held on Saturdays at Surprise Water Education Center, 16126 N. Civic Center Plz. Unless otherwise noted.
Registration is required to attend. For more information visit surpriseaz.gov/WaterConservation.
   April 11| Heat-Proofing Gardening | 9 - 10 a.m.
   April 18 | Landscape & Gardening Tips for the Low Desert | 9 - 10 a.m.
   April 25 | Watering in the Desert | 9 - 10 a.m.
   May 2 | Xeriscape Garden Tour | 9 - 10 a.m.
   May 9 | Irrigation Maintenance, Troubleshooting & Repair | 9 - 10 a.m.
   May 23 | Desert Gardens that Blooms Year-Round | 10 - 11 a.m.

“The Wall that Heals” Vietnam Veterans Memorial
The Wall That Heals is a three-quarter scale replica of the Vietnam Veterans Memorial that honors the more than three million Americans who served in the Vietnam War and bears the names of the 58,281 men and women who gave their lives in service to our country. The exhibit and Mobile Education Center are coming to Mark Coronado Park from April 9 - 12. It will be open 24 hours a day until 2 p.m. on Sunday and is free for the public to experience.

A participatory event to honor veterans who returned and struggle or who have passed followed by a solemn tribute of Taps – a moment of reflection and remembrance for all who sacrificed.

Mobile Education Center Volunteer
Engage visitors by discussing exhibit items, In Memory Honorees and Hometown Heroes. Share the history and purpose of the Mobile Education Center.
Visitor Tent – Seated Position
Assist visitors with finding names using the website or name directory. Monitor trifold inventory and answer visitor questions to the best of your ability.

Greet and assist visitors with general information. Direct guests to The Wall, Mobile Education Center and Visitor Tent. Provide mobility assistance as needed and fill in other roles when necessary.

City Expands Partnership to Help More Families Secure Affordable Healthcare
The City of Surprise Human Service & Community Vitality Department continually seeks opportunities to better meet the needs of our community members. Recently, they partnered with Helping Families in Need (HFIN) to expand appointment opportunities for local families who need assistance in gaining and navigating healthcare coverage options.
The organization assists community members with applying for AHCCCS, SNAP, Marketplace (Affordable Care Act) and other health-related resources.
New Appointment Hours:Surprise Resource Center, 12425 W. Bell Rd., Ste 124 Mondays, Wednesdays & Thursdays | 8 a.m. - 5 p.m.Fridays | 8 a.m. - 3 p.m.

Appointments are required for services. To schedule, please contact HFIN at 1.800.298.4346.
Participants are asked to have the following documentation available for their appointment:

   Driver’s license

Surprise City Hall Adds Self-service Passport Photo Booth
A new, self-service passport photo booth is now available inside City Hall. Residents and visitors who need a high‑quality passport photo now have a fast and convenient new option near the City Clerk’s Office where the City’s other passport services are housed.
Payment is quick and easy using the card reader inside the booth. The system accepts all major credit cards, as well as tap-to-pay options. The photos cost about $15.
For a small additional cost of $5, customers may also choose to have a digital copy of their passport photo emailed directly to them. This option is ideal for customers who are renewing their passports online, saving time and eliminating the need for additional photos.
Currently, the booth can only be used by adults and children who can sit independently inside a small enclosed raised booth with a height-adjustable seat. The booth cannot accommodate infant passport photos.
For more information about passport services at Surprise City Hall, visit surpriseaz.gov/Passport or contact the City Clerk’s Office at 623.222.1200.
Sign Up Now for “Alert Surprise,” the City’s Emergency Notification System
The City of Surprise has launched Alert Surprise, a text, email and/or voice call emergency notification system powered by Smart911. The system allows for one-way direct communication from the City to subscribers in potentially life-threatening situations. It’s a free service designed to keep those who live, work or visit Surprise informed and connected when it matters most.
Through Alert Surprise, subscribers can choose to add address information to receive targeted emergency alerts based on locations they choose, such as home, work or school.
Registration is simple and secure. Follow the instructions at surpriseaz.gov/AlertSurprise, or download the Smart911 app in the Google Play or Apple App Store to create a personalized safety profile. Once registered, users can customize how they receive alerts, manage multiple addresses and ensure their information is up to date.
To quickly sign up for text alerts ONLY, opt-in by texting the word SURPRISE to 67283. Please note that online signups at surpriseaz.gov/AlertSurprise allow for more notification options and account preferences.
This emergency alert service is provided by the City of Surprise at no cost to the public; however, message and data rates may apply depending on your provider and phone services. Please refer to your mobile phone's service plan for details.
For more information on the Alert Surprise system, visit surpriseaz.gov/AlertSurprise.
MARCH HIGHLIGHTS
National League of Cities and Towns
I recently traveled to Washington, D.C. to advocate on behalf of the City of Surprise’s top federal priorities, including the USDOT BUILD grant application for the Loop 303/155th Avenue traffic interchange, Congressional Directed Spending requests, and long-term water infrastructure needs.
During the visit, I met directly with key members of Arizona’s congressional delegation and federal agencies to reinforce the urgency of these initiatives. Discussions with Congressman Abe Hamadeh, staff for Congressman Juan Ciscomani, as well as Congressmen Paul Gosar and Greg Stanton focused on securing federal support for critical transportation investments, particularly the Loop 303/155th Avenue project, which is essential to address rapid growth and public safety in north Surprise.
Councilmen Patrick Duffy, Nick Haney and I also engaged with the U.S. Department of Transportation to highlight the competitiveness and regional significance of Surprise’s BUILD grant application. Conversations emphasized how the project aligns with federal priorities, including safety, economic development and infrastructure resilience.
In addition, meetings with U.S. Department of Homeland Security and U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement were highly constructive and informative. The discussions were conversational in nature and provided valuable insight into federal processes and expectations related to the proposed processing facility. This engagement allowed the City to better understand operational considerations, intergovernmental coordination needs and potential public safety and community impacts—helping position Surprise to more effectively prepare, plan and respond should the facility move forward.
During our time in D.C., we were able to spend time with Surprise Youth Council and answer questions about our experiences on Council as well as our meetings in D.C.
Lastly, we also met with utility partners EPCOR and Southwest Gas to discuss water resources, infrastructure planning and ensuring sustainable service delivery to support ongoing growth.

City Launches Surprise Arts, Brings Performances to The Vista
The City of Surprise is launching Surprise Arts, a new City-supported presenting program that brings professional performances back to The Vista Center for the Arts and expands arts and culture opportunities across the community – including new public art projects, community concerts and creative partnerships.
During the soft launch of Surprise Arts this spring, the City will begin hosting shows at The Vista, gearing up for the announcement this summer of the first full season coming in 2026-2027. These shows include:

Tickets for upcoming performances are available at surprisearts.com.

The City of Surprise Honored with JustServe City Award
I am proud to share that the City of Surprise was honored with the JustServe City Award at the City Council meeting on January 20. This recognition reflects our community’s strong commitment to volunteerism and service.
For more than a decade, JustServe has partnered with the City of Surprise Human Service & Community Vitality team to promote volunteerism and build a culture of service that strengthens our community. Through this partnership, residents have been connected to thousands of meaningful volunteer opportunities, including our annual Surprise Day of Service.
In 2023, JustServe began recognizing cities that encourage collaboration, inclusive service for all ages and backgrounds, and community driven change through the JustServe City Award. Surprise is honored to be recognized as a model JustServe City and to be nominated for the Global JustServe City Award, which will be presented at the National League of Cities this March.
Thank you to our residents, community partners and volunteers who continue to make Surprise a city that serves.
Want to join the next Day of Service? Sign up at surpriseaz.gov/DayOfService.

Surprise City Hall Adds Self-service Passport Photo Booth
A new, self-service passport photo booth is now available inside City Hall. Residents and visitors who need a high‑quality passport photo now have a fast and convenient new option near the City Clerk’s Office where the City’s other passport services are housed.
Payment is quick and easy using the card reader inside the booth. The system accepts all major credit cards, as well as tap-to-pay options. The photos cost about $15.
For a small additional cost of $5, customers may also choose to have a digital copy of their passport photo emailed directly to them. This option is ideal for customers who are renewing their passports online, saving time and eliminating the need for additional photos.
Currently, the booth can only be used by adults and children who can sit independently inside a small enclosed raised booth with a height-adjustable seat. The booth cannot accommodate infant passport photos.
For more information about passport services at Surprise City Hall, visit surpriseaz.gov/Passport or contact the City Clerk’s Office at 623.222.1200.
PSA Yield Signs
The City of Surprise is launching a campaign to remind drivers to yield on flashing yellow arrow traffic signals.
These signals feature a flashing yellow arrow in addition to the standard red, yellow and green arrows. When illuminated, the flashing yellow arrow allows waiting motorists to make a left-hand turn after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians in the crosswalk. They are designed to help with traffic flow, creating more opportunities to make a left turn with the flashing yellow left-turn arrow than with the traditional three-arrow, red, yellow and green indications.
While flashing yellow arrows can increase traffic flow, it is important that drivers wait for a safe gap in oncoming traffic before turning. That is why the City is launching an educational campaign with the slogan, “When the yellow arrow flashes, yield to avoid crashes.”
The campaign includes a Public Service Announcement and new road signs that will be placed in medians approaching 10 intersections throughout Surprise where flashing yellow traffic signals are used.
Watch the Public Service Announcement.

Sign Up Now for “Alert Surprise,” the City’s New Emergency Notification System
The City of Surprise has launched Alert Surprise, a new text, email and/or voice call emergency notification system powered by Smart911. The system allows for one-way direct communication from the City to subscribers in potentially life-threatening situations. It’s a free service designed to keep those who live, work or visit Surprise informed and connected when it matters most.
Through Alert Surprise, subscribers can choose to add address information to receive targeted emergency alerts based on locations they choose, such as home, work or school.
Registration is simple and secure. Follow the instructions at surpriseaz.gov/AlertSurprise, or download the Smart911 app in the Google Play or Apple App Store to create a personalized safety profile. Once registered, users can customize how they receive alerts, manage multiple addresses and ensure their information is up to date.
To quickly sign up for text alerts ONLY, opt-in by texting the word SURPRISE to 67283. Please note that online signups at surpriseaz.gov/AlertSurprise allow for more notification options and account preferences.
This emergency alert service is provided by the City of Surprise at no cost to the public; however, message and data rates may apply depending on your provider and phone services. Please refer to your mobile phone's service plan for details.
For more information on the Alert Surprise system, visit surpriseaz.gov/AlertSurprise.

CURRENT EVENTS
City Council Meetings
The next City Council Meetings is on December 16at 6 p.m. The public is welcome to attend in person, or you can tune in on surpriseaz.gov/SurpriseTV, the City of Surprise Government Facebook page at facebook.com/CityOfSurprise or on Cox channel 11.
Surprise Party & Sparkling Surprise
Celebrate the season with family and friends at the annual Surprise Party, a free, two-night event filled with festive activities, entertainment and holiday fun for all ages! Enjoy a winter wonderland complete with a snow party, mesmerizing drone shows, glowing hot air balloons, thrilling skydivers and special performances. New this year, lace up your skates and glide across the brand-new roller rink, available both nights!
Savor delicious treats from food trucks, explore the vendor village, let the kids have fun in the kid zone and capture a memorable photo with Santa in Santa’s Workshop. Don’t miss Storytime at Surprise Regional Library and more exciting holiday activities.
Come celebrate with us at Surprise Community Park – 15960 N. Bullard Ave. – for an unforgettable holiday experience!
Friday, December 5 • 5 - 9 p.m.
The festivities kick off at 5 p.m. with DJ music, family-friendly activities and lively entertainment! Catch a special surprise performance to set the holiday mood.
At 6:30 p.m., join the Mayor and City Council for the annual Tree Lighting Ceremony, illuminating a beautiful tree. Then, look to the skies for a thrilling show by the Aerial Extreme Skydiving Team, followed by a dazzling fireworks display to end the night.
Friday’s event also launches Sparkling Surprise, a brilliant light display transforming the entire Surprise Community Park, and featuring more lights and lands than ever before. This event runs nightly from 5:30 p.m. to 10 p.m. through December 28.
Saturday, December 6 • 5 - 9 p.m.
Saturday brings even more magic with two captivating drone shows featuring 200 LED-lit drones, creating holiday animations set to music! The evening continues with a hot air balloon glow, live ice sculpting and real snow for a magical evening under the stars.
This event is sponsored by American Furniture Warehouse. Canned food donations are appreciated for Operation Santa, brought to you by Sanderson Ford and Sanderson Lincoln.
For more event details, including event maps, parking information and road closures, please visit surpriseaz.gov/SurpriseParty or contact 623.222.2000.

Cookies and Crafts with Santa
The City of Surprise is excited to present Cookies and Crafts with Santa on December 14! Families and children are invited to join us for a day filled with festive activities, heartwarming moments and a special visit from Santa Claus himself.
This fun-filled event offers six time slots between 11 a.m. - 7 p.m., so everyone can find the perfect time to enjoy the holiday magic and create lasting memories. Cookies and Crafts with Santa will be held at the Parks & Recreation Office, located at 15960 N. Bullard Ave.
Registration is required, with a fee of $5 per person for Surprise residents and $7 per person for non-residents. Space is limited, so make sure to register early!
Click here to register for the Cookies & Crafts with Santa, or visit surpriseaz.gov/SurpriseRec and search through “Special Programs.”
Step into the Magic of Santa’s Workshop

This festive experience will take place at the Surprise Stadium Team Shop, located at 15930 N. Bullard Ave. The workshop is open to the community for three magical nights on December 10, 11 and 12, from 5:30 to 8:30 p.m.
This free event features Santa’s jolly spirit, a sweet treat and holiday-themed coloring activities for kids. Please be sure to bring your own camera to capture family smiles, as no professional photographers will be on-site.
For more information, visit surpriseaz.gov/SpecialEvents.

Fire First Fridays
The Surprise Fire-Medical Department is offering free life-saving knowledge and training. These classes aim to provide attendees with basic emergency response training in a comfortable and stress-free environment so community members will know how to start helping while waiting for emergency crews.
CPR Classes–Take a free non-certifying CPR class. Participants will learn the basics of CPR, including chest compressions, AED use and rescue breathing.Classes are typically the first Friday of each even-numbered month,but the next session will be on December 12.
Stop the Bleed & Narcan Classes–Participants will learn the basic skills needed to save someone in the event of a deep-penetrating injury and the proper technique needed to deliver Narcan. Classes are on the first Friday of each odd-numbered month. The next class is on January 9 due to the holiday week.
To register and get more information, visit surpriseaz.gov/FireFirstFridays. Please note, registration forms open two weeks before the class date. The maximum class size is 40 people for each session. Participants must be 12 years old or older, and a registered adult must accompany attendees aged 12 - 17.
Live at Asante
LIVE at Asante is a free and casual outdoor performance series at Asante Library for all ages. This America’s 250th celebration series runs the third Thursday of each month through May 2026 from 6 - 8 p.m. at Asante Library, 16755 W. Vereda Solana Dr. Each event will feature a different genre and include a brief history of its importance in America’s music. Featuring live music provided by On Stage Now, free arts activities and food trucks! Bring your lawn chair, your friends and your family for an evening of entertainment. RSVP recommended at surpriseaz.gov/ArtsSignUp.

Maricopa Water District Celebration
On Friday, November 7, I had the honor of attending the Maricopa Water District’s 100-year celebration, where I presented a proclamation recognizing the District’s historic contributions, pioneering spirit and enduring commitment to responsible water management and community service. For a century, the Maricopa Water District has played a vital role in supporting Arizona’s growth and long-term sustainability, and it was a privilege to join in celebrating this remarkable milestone.

NEWS & UPDATES
Spring Training Season at Surprise Stadium
Spring training games are back!With 31 games on the schedule and two World Baseball Classic exhibition matchups added to the lineup, fans can look forward to an exciting mix of MLB talent, rising stars and international competition. Opening day is February 20 and games run through March 21.
Also look forward to the MLB Spring Breakout event on March 20, featuring the Kansas City Royals vs. Texas Rangers. This special game will showcase the next generation of Major League talent, highlighting baseball’s brightest prospects.
Individual game tickets for Spring Training at Surprise Stadium will go on sale online atSurpriseStadium.com on December 2,at 10 a.m. (AZ time).
Additional details regarding the upcoming season including promotional schedules, special events and ticket offers will be announced in the coming weeks. Fill out ourticket information form to be alerted when they go live. You can also stay up to date by visitingSurpriseStadium.com or signing up forSports & Tourism notifications.
Competition for Local Musicians – Winner Plays at Out of the Park Music Fest!
The City of Surprise is calling on local musicians to showcase their talent at Play Your Way to the Park – a new, live music competition where selected up-and-coming bands or solo artists will rock the stage for a shot to perform at the 2026Out of the Park Music Fest.
Play Your Way to the Park will take place Saturday, February 7, 2026, at 6 p.m. at The Vista Center for the Arts. The event is free and open to the public with a ticket reservation. Reserve your spot now atsurpriseaz.gov/ArtsSignUp.
The winning act will earn a $1,000 artist fee and will open Out of the Park Music Fest on March 21, 2026, at Mark Coronado Park, following the Kansas City Royals vs. Colorado Rockies Surprise Spring Training game. The top three acts will receive studio recording time throughOn Stage Now’s Recording Assistance Program.
Musicians from across the Metro-Phoenix region are invited to apply. We welcome all genres, all ages and all sounds that get the crowd on their feet.
How to ApplyMusicians can apply atsurpriseaz.gov/Arts. Applications are open now, and the submission deadline is December 14, 2025, at 11:59 p.m.Artists are encouraged to submit early to ensure consideration. Artists will be notified by the selection panel by early January if they are moving on to the live competition.
Winter Adult Sports Registration Opens Soon
The City of Surprise Parks & Recreation Department is gearing up for adult softball, kickball, volleyball, basketball and flag football leagues!
Returning teams from Surprise Winter 2026 Adult Sports Leagues may register online now, or by calling the Parks & Recreation office at 623.222.2000 between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m.
Starting December 3, registration will open to non-returning and new teams. Teams may register over the phone, at the Parks & Recreation office on 15960 N. Bullard Ave or online atsurpriseaz.gov/SurpriseRec.
Free Block Party Trailer Rentals Available
What better way to get to know your neighbors than by throwing a great party? Surprise offers free rentals of a Block Party Trailer, and we’ll even deliver it to you!
The block party trailer includes chairs, tables, coolers, canopy tents, sports equipment, games, music and public announcement system and a generator. City staff will deliver the trailer and be present during the event.
Applicants may also receive a reimbursement of up to $150 that can be used toward qualifying expenses to help pay for food or party amenities and entertainment. Approved applicants will receive a Block Party Kit, which includes flyers to help spread the word about your party.
Reservations are required at least two weeks in advance and are granted on a first come, first served basis. Once approved, you can start planning your party!
Learn more at surpriseaz.gov/BlockParty.
Surprise Libraries to Celebrate Semiquincentennial with New Reading Program
The Surprise Public Library System is excited to announce a new reading program in celebration of America’s 250 th – Stars & Stories. The program will run until July 4, 2026.
Readers will be awarded fun badges and prizes, based on minutes read. The goal is to read 1,776 minutes over the next nine months in honor of the signing of the Declaration of Independence on July 4, 1776. Prizes will include books, American flags, challenge coins and patriotic swag!
Visit the Stars & Stories reading program webpage to register and learn more about the program: 1776.surprisepubliclibrary.gov.
Check out more Surprise America 250 library events at SurprisePublicLIbrary.gov/Programs, search America 250.

Household Hazardous Waste Collection Appointments
Surprise has officially moved the Household Hazardous Waste (HHW) Collection Program to a monthly appointment system. Surprise residents can request an appointment for HHW collection pickup right from their driveway during one week each month.
The collection of these materials is split into two types and scheduled separately. One pick-up is for electronic HHW, including flat-screen TVs, computers and cell phones. Another is for all other HHW.
NEW pickup periods through June 2026:
   December 2025 – No Pickups
   January 2026 – No Pickups
   February 9-12, 2026
   March 9-12, 2026
   April 13-16, 2026
   May 11-14, 2026
   June 8-11, 2026
For more information on acceptable items, visit surpriseaz.gov/HHW.
To schedule a collection, residents can use the My Surprise app or call customer service at 623.222.1920. All scheduling is based on availability and is first-come, first-served.

The annual event returns to Mark Coronado Park (15850 N. Bullard Ave.) on Saturday, March 21, 2026, from 2 - 10 p.m.
Tickets available NOW at OutOfTheParkMusicFest.com/Tickets. If you’re looking for the VIP experience, get your tickets now, because they will sell out!

District 5 Newsletter November 2025
Dear D5 residents,
November is a month that reminds us just how much we have to be thankful for. This month, we have two perfect opportunities to come together and celebrate what makes our city so special. On November 11, we’ll honor the men and women who have served our country at the Veterans Day Parade—a meaningful tradition that reflects the deep respect and gratitude we share for our veterans. Later in the month, the SciTech Festival returns, offering hands-on learning and discovery for all ages, and highlighting the innovation and curiosity that thrive right here in Surprise.
As we enter this season of gratitude, I encourage you to join your neighbors, support one another and take part in these events that showcase the heart of our community.

District 5 Newsletter October 2025
Dear D5 residents,
October is here, and it’s the perfect time to get outside, enjoy cooler nights and connect with the community. This year is especially exciting as we welcome the very first Desert Fall Festival, a brand-new tradition you won’t want to miss. From festive activities to family fun, this inaugural event is a great way to celebrate autumn in Surprise.
We’re also looking forward to one of our community’s favorite traditions, the Surprise Fiesta Grande at Mark Coronado Park. This vibrant celebration of culture, food and entertainment is a wonderful way to connect with our community and experience the rich diversity that makes Surprise so special.
I hope you’ll take advantage of these opportunities to come together, enjoy the season, and celebrate everything that makes our city such a great place to live.

Tax Preparation Volunteers Needed
Volunteers are needed to help low to moderate-income families and individuals prepare their taxes. The Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program is sponsored by the IRS and provides free tax return preparation services through a large network of short-term volunteers.
Volunteers are not required to have prior tax preparation experience; training will be provided. There is a large variety of volunteer opportunities within the VITA program, including:
Tax Preparer – Prepares basic or advanced 1040 tax returns and related schedules for eligible taxpayers. Interviews taxpayers to determine if all income, deductions and allowable credits are claimed.
Greeter/Screener – Greets taxpayers to create a welcoming atmosphere and performs the screening process of all taxpayers prior to moving them to the tax preparer.
Interpreter – Provides bilingual assistance for Spanish speaking clients seeking tax preparation services.
For those interested in learning more, VITA information sessions are available at Surprise Resource Center, Riverboat Bingo and online. Sessions begin September 11, and go through November 6. To see dates and to register online, use the sign-up link.

Locations:
   Surprise Resource Center - 12425 W. Bell Rd., Building A, Suite 124
   Hollyhock Library - 15844 N. Hollyhock St.
   Surprise Regional Library - 16089 N. Bullard Ave.
   Asante Library - 16755 W. Vereda Solana Dr.
The Maricopa Association of Governments (MAG) coordinates an interactive map with more than 200 locations countywide where people can find indoor cooling and respite centers, hydration stations or donation sites. The network is available at azmag.gov/HeatRelief.
Anyone can experience heat illness or death, regardless of age, health, fitness level or how long they’ve lived in Arizona. The three stages of heat illness are heat cramps, heat exhaustion and heat stroke. Learn the signs and symptoms of heat related illness and when it’s time to seek medical attention on the Maricopa County Department of Public Health website.
Here are some tips to stay heat safe:
   Stay hydrated; drink water before you feel thirsty.
   If you’re sweating heavily or active for long periods, consider drinks with electrolytes to help replace salts and minerals.
   Wear lightweight, light-colored clothing and a wide-brimmed hat.
   Use air conditioning whenever possible. Fans alone are not effective when temperatures exceed 95°F.
   Check on older adults and people living alone often. If something increases their risk, like losing power or air conditioning, you can help them move to a safer, cooler place.
   Never leave children, pets or anyone else in a parked car.

The City of Surprise is partnering with Maricopa County’s Human Services Department and Wildfire Arizona to offer a free diaper support program for families with children.
The program is available to qualifying families to ensure babies and toddlers stay clean, dry and healthy. Eligible families can receive up to 150 diapers per child each month. While supplies last.
The Diaper Hub is available at the Surprise Resource Center, 12425 W. Bell Rd. From now through October 2025, the program will be held on the second Wednesday of the month from 8:30 - 10:30 a.m. and on the fourth Wednesday of the month from 3 - 5 p.m.

Legal Kiosk Now Available to Residents
The Surprise Resource Center has been selected as one of only eight locations in the state to host a self-service legal kiosk through a partnership with Community Legal Services.
The kiosk is designed as an easy-to-use portal that helps users learn about civil legal issues, find court information and forms, access legal aid providers by appointment and locate community resources.
The kiosk inside the Surprise Resource Center is available to the public during regular hours: Mondays and Tuesdays from 8 a.m. - 5 p.m., Wednesdays and Thursdays from 8 a.m. - 7 p.m., Fridays from 8 a.m. - 3 p.m. and the third Saturday of the month from 9 a.m. - 1 p.m.
The Community Legal Services’ Kiosk Project is part of their commitment to bringing access to legal information, resources and services to community members in areas where travel to one of their offices is too burdensome.
For more information or to set up an appointment to use the legal kiosk, please call the Surprise Resource Center at 623.222.1600.
Heat Relief Stations
The City of Surprise is a Heat Relief Network partner. Through September 30, Surprise has several locations providing free hydration and indoor refuge stations for those needing a place to cool off during the year's hottest months.
Locations:
   Surprise Resource Center - 12425 W. Bell Rd., Building A, Suite 124
   Hollyhock Library - 15844 N. Hollyhock St.
   Surprise Regional Library - 16089 N. Bullard Ave.
   Asante Library - 16755 W. Vereda Solana Dr.
The Maricopa Association of Governments (MAG) coordinates an interactive map with more than 200 locations countywide where people can find indoor cooling and respite centers, hydration stations or donation sites. The network is available at azmag.gov/HeatRelief.
Anyone can experience heat illness or death, regardless of age, health, fitness level or how long they’ve lived in Arizona. The three stages of heat illness are heat cramps, heat exhaustion and heat stroke. Learn the signs and symptoms of heat related illness and when it’s time to seek medical attention on the Maricopa County Department of Public Health website.
Here are some tips to stay heat safe:
   Stay hydrated; drink water before you feel thirsty.
   If you’re sweating heavily or active for long periods, consider drinks with electrolytes to help replace salts and minerals.
   Wear lightweight, light-colored clothing and a wide-brimmed hat.
   Use air conditioning whenever possible. Fans alone are not effective when temperatures exceed 95°F.
   Check on older adults and people living alone often. If something increases their risk, like losing power or air conditioning, you can help them move to a safer, cooler place.
   Never leave children, pets or anyone else in a parked car.

Mayor Kevin Sartor proclaimed June 8 - 15 as Monsoon Awareness Week in Surprise and encourages all our residents to be prepared for this unpredictable and severe weather season. Monsoon season is typically observed from June 15 - September 30, and brings increased thunderstorm and lightning activity along with the possibility of high winds, dust storms and flash floods. While the severity of weather can vary each season, Surprise residents should have a monsoon safety plan. Read more:

Apraxia Awareness Proclamation
On Wednesday, May 14, I had the honor of presenting a proclamation recognizing Apraxia Awareness Day here in Surprise.
Childhood apraxia of speech is a rare and complex neurological disorder that makes it difficult for children to clearly and consistently speak. It affects about one in 1,000 children and requires early intervention, ongoing support and a great deal of perseverance.
By raising awareness, we can support families, educators and therapists working hard to help these incredible kids find their voice.

Household Hazardous Waste Collection Appointments
Surprise is officially moving the Household Hazardous Waste (HHW) Collection Program to a monthly appointment system. Through November, Surprise residents can request an appointment for HHW collection pickup right from their driveway during one week each month.
The collection of these materials is split into two types and scheduled separately. One pick-up is for electronic HHW, including flat-screen TVs, computers and cell phones. Another is for all other HHW.

   May 11-14, 2026
   June 8-11, 2026

Designing a Water Harvesting Landscape

Surprise Fire-Medical Summer Camp Opportunity
The Surprise Fire-Medical Department is excited to offer a one-week program for high school girls, aged 14-18, providing a unique opportunity to learn directly from female firefighter mentors.
Camp Ignite was developed to help teen girls learn and practice skills to help them respond in emergency situations. They will also take away life skills that will instill confidence, leadership and the ability to act under pressure. This program aims to empower girls to discover their inner strength, accomplish hard things and learn they are stronger than they believe and tougher than they ever imagined.
Participants can:
   Receive CPR/First Aid/AED certification
   Workout in firefighter gear
   Learn firefighting skills
   Work on team-building skills and interpersonal skills
Camp Details:Camp will take place on June 23 - 26 from 8 a.m. - 4 p.m. at Fire Station 304, 24550 N. 163rd Ave. On June 27, camp will run from 8 a.m. - noon, and an awards ceremony will occur that evening. Participants will also need to attend Orientation Day on June 22 anytime between noon and 5 p.m. to get fitted for turnouts, pick up camp t-shirts and meet camp leaders.

The Volunteer Income Tax Assistance Program (VITA) offers IRS trained and certified volunteers to assist households making $67,000 or less in preparing their tax returns.

Free Smoke Alarms for Deaf and Hard of Hearing
The City of Surprise Fire-Medical Department and Surprise Firefighter Charities are partnering with the Arizona Burn Foundation, Arizona Commission for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing and the Red Cross to install free deaf and hard of hearing smoke alarm systems.
These specialized smoke alarms have a strobe light and bed shaker paired with a receiver to help provide an early warning signal in case of a fire for anyone who is deaf or hard of hearing.
The installation of these free smoke detectors is available to Surprise residents by appointment only. Appointments are limited, participants are asked to sign up no later than April 7. To make an appointment, please visit abf.azburn.org/DHHappointment.
If you have any questions or would like more information about this installation event, please visit azburn.org, call 602.230.2041, or email programs@azburn.org.

When Disaster Strikes: Prepare, Act, Survive Course
The City is hosting a course designed to equip individuals with the essential skills needed to respond to a disaster when emergency responders may not be immediately available. In this free course, participants 18 and older will learn how to create a family preparedness plan, perform self-rescue, conduct basic search and light rescue operations, and understand the recovery process following an emergency. While the course does not certify individuals to serve as professional emergency responders, it provides vital knowledge and skills for managing immediate disaster impacts.

City of Surprise Awarded Reaccreditation with American Public Works Association
The City of Surprise was recently honored to receive full accreditation by the American Public Works Association (APWA) for the second time. This four-year accreditation formally verifies and recognizes that the City’s Public Works, Transportation, Water Resource Management and Community Development departments are in full compliance with the recommended management practices of the APWA’s Public Works Management Practices Manual.
The City pursued this second accreditation to promote excellence in the operation and management of a public works agency, its programs and employees. Accreditation is designed to assist the City in the continuous improvement of operations and management and in providing a valid and objective evaluation of City programs.
APWA’s accreditation process has several significant steps, including an internal review of the City’s practices combined with a comparison of the recommended practices contained in the manual; identifying areas needing improvement and bringing all practices into compliance with the recommended practices; a site visit for review and evaluation; and a final review by the Accreditation Council.
The City earned APWA Accreditation for the first time in 2021, becoming one of only six agencies in the State of Arizona to receive this prestigious accreditation

City Council names Jack Hastings as Vice Mayor
The Surprise City Council selected District 5 Councilmember Jack Hastings to serve as Vice Mayor at their January 21 meeting.
Per city code, City Council must select a new Vice Mayor to serve on an annual basis no later than January 30. The Vice Mayor acts on behalf of the Mayor in the event of absence.
Hastings was first elected to the Surprise City Council in 2020 and was re-elected in 2022 to a four-year term.
He serves on several City Council committees, including the Performance Evaluation Leaders and Tourism Fund subcommittees. He is Chair of both the Education and Rules committees.
Hastings has lived in Surprise for 20 years. He received a Bachelor’s Degree from Arizona State University and a Master’s Degree from Northern Arizona University. He currently works for the Dysart Unified School District.

Diabetes Awareness Proclamation
Mayor Skip Hall officially proclaimed November as Diabetes Awareness Month in Surprise on November 4 at the City Council meeting.
Diabetes Awareness Month is recognized annually each November to increase awareness of risk factors, symptoms and the different types of diabetes. According to the American Diabetes Association, over 38 million people in the United States have diabetes.
The color blue is used on November 14 to show support and provide community awareness and encourage recognition and effort to change the future of this disease.
The proclamation was accepted by members of Sun Health and the City’s Human Service & Community Vitality Director Seth Dyson.

Domestic Violence Awareness Proclamation
Mayor Skip Hall officially proclaimed October as Domestic Violence Awareness Month at the City Council Meeting on October 1. As part of the proclamation, Mayor Hall urged the community to raise awareness about this issue and work together to eliminate domestic violence in our city.
The color purple symbolizes the national effort to support Domestic Violence Awareness Month (DVAM). During this year’s national “Wear Purple Day” on October 17, City staff wore purple, and our community was encouraged to take part in this visible way to show support for victims and the numerous organizations that provide advocacy and assistance to them. Our community saw both Surprise City Hall and the City’s Public Safety Building lit purple each evening during the entire month in support of DVAM.
If you or someone you know is a victim of domestic violence, you are encouraged to call the National Domestic Violence Hotline at 1.800.799.SAFE (7233) or visit TheHotline.org.

Public Art Coming Soon
Public art is coming to the West Valley in a whole new way! Municipal public art programs across six cities – Buckeye, Goodyear, Litchfield Park, Peoria, Surprise and Tolleson – are joining forces with WESTMARC, the Western Maricopa Coalition, to launch a vibrant new initiative called "iHola! West Valley." This exciting partnership will bring a series of temporary, site-specific art installations created by local artists to various locations throughout the West Valley.
iHola! stands for, “Highlighting Our Local Arts,” and aims to connect West Valley cities and residents to arts and culture resources, spotlight the abundance of arts and culture activities and opportunities in this thriving region, and provide mentorship for local artists to enter the field of public art. Once art is installed, the goal is to create a map of locations, paving the way for future bus tours. This allows residents to discover new art experiences and explore different aspects of the West Valley's artistic landscape.
A Call to Artists
iHola! West Valley is now accepting applications from Arizona-based artists across various disciplines to create temporary, site-specific art projects in a variety of West Valley locations. This initiative aims to foster a deeper connection between our cities and residents. Interested artists can submit proposals by visiting westmarc.org/HOLA by the deadline of October 1, 2024.
Neighborhood Grant Program Application
The Neighborhood Grant Program provides eligible neighborhoods with small grants to fund neighborhood events, functions or community improvements.An opportunity to encourage community engagement and further build strong relationships with Surprise's communities.
To be considered, projects must meet the following program criteria:
   Provide a public benefit
   Result in a product, which benefits a neighborhood or the larger Surprise community
   Be feasible for completion by June 2025
   Involve the benefiting neighborhood in project identification, planning and execution
   Emphasize neighborhood self-help; be educational or community-building
   Nonprofit 501(c)3 organizations are ineligible
Additionally, a minimum of five neighbors within the residential area must sign the application and support the idea; neighborhoods governed by a homeowner association (HOA) must have HOA approval, if required.
Interested communities should deliver the complete Neighborhood Grant Program application to City Hall, attention Jodi Tas, by 5 p.m. on Friday, October 11.
For more information about the grant, visit surpriseaz.gov/NGP or contact jodi.tas@surpriseaz.gov or 623.222.1330.
Household Hazardous Waste
Surprise is rolling out a new Household Hazardous Waste (HHW) Collection Program. Instead of offering collection events three times a year, a pilot program will allow residents to schedule collections monthly. The pilot program will run from September through November 2024.
During this pilot program, HHW collection will be split into two types and scheduled separately. One pick-up will occur for just electronic HHW, including TVs, computers and cell phones. Another pick-up will occur for all other HHW, including pool chemicals, pesticides and solvents. Crews will schedule up to 50 collections for just electronic HHW materials per month on the first day of the collection week (Mondays) for residents north and south of Bell Road.
All other HHW materials will be collected Monday through Thursday, for one week per month, with two days reserved for residents south of Bell Road and two days reserved for residents north of Bell Road. Crews will be able to complete about 40 scheduled HHW collections per day.
Starting Monday, August 26, residents can start requesting an appointment for collections during this three-month pilot period. To schedule a collection, residents can use the My Surprise app or call Customer Service at 623.222.1920. All scheduling is based on availability and is first come, first served.
On the date of your scheduled collection, all acceptable items must be placed outside on the driveway and up against the garage by 6 a.m. on the scheduled collection date. Properly packaged, sealed and labeled items will be picked up between 6 a.m. - 4 p.m. DO NOT place any hazardous waste on sidewalks or curbside.
For more information, visit surpriseaz.gov/HHW.
Keep in mind this is not a bulk trash appointment. Residents can schedule bulk trash and appliance pickup services anytime throughout the year. See a full list of acceptable bulk trash items online at surpriseaz.gov/BulkTrash.

NEWS & UPDATES
Heat Relief Network
Arizona recognizes Heat Awareness Week May 6 - 10, to raise awareness about heat risk, promote indoor and outdoor heat safety and encourage those at greater risk of serious illness or death to connect to cooling centers and other resources.
The Heat Relief Network is a regional partnership of the Maricopa Association of Governments (MAG), municipalities, nonprofit organizations, the faith-based community and businesses. Each year, MAG coordinates the mapping of the Heat Relief Network, a network of partners providing hydration stations, refuge locations and water donation sites throughout the valley with the goal of preventing heat-related illnesses and deaths among vulnerable populations. For more information visit Heat Relief Network - Maricopa Association of Governments (azmag.gov)
